displayTitle: Where Does It Sit? Plot, Size, Report

beats.narration: An angle-beam echo gives one number, sound path. Trigonometry turns it into an exact position in the weld.

payload.title: WHERE DOES IT SIT?
payload.sub: Sound path into position, position into a report.
payload.panelLabel: SHEAR WAVE // FIRST LEG TO SECOND

beats.narration: Ask the leg first. Surface distance is sound path times sine. Depth is sound path times cosine; after the bounce, two-T minus that.
A sound weld still echoes. If a signal plots onto a metal surface, suspect geometry, the root or the crown, before you ever call a flaw.
Maximize the echo, read it as a percent of the curve, size length by the six decibel drop, locate it from the datum, record the leg.

payload.heading: Size it, then write it down

beats.narration: The report's test never changes: hand it over, and another technician finds the same reflector within a probe-width. Plot honestly, size honestly.

payload.sub: Another tech re-finds it from the paper alone.
